name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value greater than the original input

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The result of align_to_usize should always be aligned to b; failing this means the function is wrong

name: aligned_contradicts_modulus
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_contradicts_modulus(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr, size),
    ensures
        addr % size != 0,
{
}
reason: If aligned returns true, the modulus must be zero; entailment here means the spec is self-contradictory

name: align_gap_exceeds_block
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_gap_exceeds_block(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a >= b,
    ensures
        a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
{
}
reason: The difference a - align_to_usize(a,b) equals a%b which must be strictly less than b; a gap >= b means over-alignment

name: align_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_5_align_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
{
}
reason: Aligning an already-aligned value should be a no-op; failure of idempotence would indicate a broken alignment function
